response: Emily divides the 24 residents into 4 groups since each group holds 6 participants. Each of these 4 groups receives 3 initial writing prompts, totaling 12 prompts for the start of the session. The 10 extra prompts are shared equally among the 4 groups, meaning each group gets 2.5 prompts if divided mathematically, but in a practical library of resources, we count the total pool of 10 extra items available for the workshop. Adding the 12 initial prompts to the 10 extra prompts gives a total of 22 writing prompts needed for the entire session. This collection serves as a small library of creative inspiration for the residents. The nurse ensures this library is well-stocked to support every participant's needs. The END.
